name: Geraint
description: Geraint, son of Erbin, is a prominent knight featured in Arthurian legends, particularly in Welsh and French romances. He is often depicted as a noble and valiant warrior, known for his strength, chivalry, and unwavering loyalty to King Arthur. Geraint's story frequently revolves around themes of love, honor, and proving one's worth. He is a complex character, sometimes portrayed as overly proud or jealous, but ultimately redeemed by his courageous actions and devotion to his wife, Enid. His adventures often involve quests to defend the innocent, defeat formidable adversaries, and uphold the ideals of the Round Table. Geraint's tale highlights the importance of trust and communication within relationships, as well as the challenges faced by knights in maintaining their honor and reputation. He embodies the chivalric code, demonstrating bravery, courtesy, and respect for women. Geraint's legacy is one of a skilled knight who overcomes personal flaws to become a true hero, leaving a lasting impact on Arthurian lore and inspiring generations with his tales of valor and romance. His narrative explores the intricacies of courtly love and the duties of a knight in a world of magic and adventure, making him a compelling figure in the rich tapestry of Arthurian mythology. He is a symbol of the ideal knight, striving for perfection and ultimately finding redemption through love and service.
subtitle: Valiant Knight of Arthur's Court
achievements: Defeated the Knight of the Sparrowhawk
Won the tournament for Enid's hand
Served King Arthur with loyalty and courage

keyMyths.title: The Sparrowhawk Tournament
keyMyths.description: Geraint participates in a tournament where the prize is a sparrowhawk. He defeats the arrogant knight holding the hawk, winning it for Enid and proving his worth.

keyMyths.title: Geraint and Enid
keyMyths.description: Geraint marries Enid but becomes overly suspicious of her loyalty. Through a series of trials and adventures, Enid proves her faithfulness and Geraint learns to trust her.

keyMyths.title: The Adventure with the Giants
keyMyths.description: Geraint encounters giants terrorizing a local population and, with his strength and courage, defeats them, restoring peace and earning the gratitude of the people.

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.description: A collection of early Welsh tales, including the tale of Geraint son of Erbin.
sources.text: A collection of early Welsh tales, including the tale of Geraint son of Erbin.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.description: A French romance featuring a similar story to Geraint and Enid.
sources.text: A French romance featuring a similar story to Geraint and Enid.

associations.name: Chivalry
associations.significance: Represents the knightly code of conduct, emphasizing honor, courtesy, and bravery.

associations.name: Strength
associations.significance: Symbolizes physical prowess and martial skill, essential for a knight in battle.

associations.name: Love
associations.significance: Represents the romantic element in his story, often linked to his relationship with Enid.

associations.name: Honor
associations.significance: Reflects the importance of maintaining one's reputation and integrity.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Highlights his dedication to King Arthur and his kingdom.

associations.name: Armor
associations.significance: Represents his identity as a knight and his readiness for battle.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both are renowned for their strength and undertaking perilous quests.
Shares a similar warrior ethos and undergoes trials to prove his worth.
Embodies the archetype of a valiant hero facing challenging adversaries.

worshipPractices.name: None
worshipPractices.description: As a figure of Arthurian romance, Geraint was not subject to any formal worship practices. He was a literary figure, celebrated in stories and poems.

cultural.modernLegacy: Geraint remains a popular figure in Arthurian literature and media, embodying the ideals of chivalry and courage. His story continues to be retold and reinterpreted in modern novels, films, and other artistic works, inspiring audiences with his noble character and exciting adventures.

extendedContent.title: Early Life
extendedContent.content: Geraint, son of Erbin, is introduced as a prince from Dumnonia (present-day Devon and Cornwall). His lineage connects him to a region with a strong Celtic heritage, setting the stage for his integration into Arthurian lore. Details of his upbringing are scarce, but he is generally portrayed as a well-bred and noble young man, destined for knighthood. His early life likely involved training in arms and horsemanship, preparing him for the challenges and responsibilities of a knight. The historical context of post-Roman Britain provides a backdrop of political instability and warfare, shaping Geraint's character as a protector of his people. His initial portrayal emphasizes his potential for greatness, hinting at the heroic deeds he will later accomplish in the service of King Arthur. This foundation establishes him as a figure of promise and nobility within the Arthurian world.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests
extendedContent.content: Geraint is best known for his quest to defend the honor of Queen Guinevere and later, his own honor after suspecting his wife, Enid, of infidelity. The first quest involves avenging an insult to the Queen by defeating a mysterious knight who had been terrorizing Arthur's court. This adventure showcases Geraint's prowess in combat and his unwavering loyalty to Arthur. His second, more personal quest, tests his trust in Enid and his ability to overcome his own pride. This journey involves a series of trials and challenges, where Enid's faithfulness and wisdom are repeatedly proven. These quests highlight Geraint's growth as a character, demonstrating his capacity to learn from his mistakes and to value the virtues of love and trust. His adventures are not merely displays of physical strength, but also explorations of moral and emotional complexities.

extendedContent.title: Legacy
extendedContent.content: Geraint's legacy lies in his embodiment of the Arthurian ideal of chivalry and his complex portrayal as a flawed but ultimately noble hero. He is remembered for his bravery, his skill in combat, and his unwavering loyalty to King Arthur. His story, particularly his relationship with Enid, explores themes of love, trust, and the challenges of maintaining honor in the face of suspicion and doubt. Geraint's character has been adapted and reinterpreted in numerous works of literature, art, and popular culture, ensuring his continued presence in the Arthurian canon. He serves as a reminder that even the most valiant knights are not immune to human frailties, and that true heroism lies in overcoming one's own weaknesses and striving for moral excellence. His story continues to resonate with audiences, offering valuable lessons about the importance of trust, communication, and forgiveness in relationships.

shortDescription: Geraint is a valiant knight in Arthurian legend, renowned for his strength, chivalry, and loyalty. His story explores themes of love, honor, and proving one's worth.
